title: Cannot build v0.1.40 on OL7
body:
```
[root@my-computer] $GOHOME/src/github.com/containers/skopeo# yum -y install gpgme-devel libassuan-devel btrfs-progs-devel device-mapper-devel
Loaded plugins: versionlock
Package gpgme-devel-1.3.2-5.el7.x86_64 already installed and latest version
Package libassuan-devel-2.1.0-3.el7.x86_64 already installed and latest version
Package btrfs-progs-devel-4.15.1-1.el7.x86_64 already installed and latest version
Package 7:device-mapper-devel-1.02.158-2.0.1.el7.x86_64 already installed and latest version
Nothing to do
[root@my-computer] $GOHOME/src/github.com/containers/skopeo # make binary-local
GO111MODULE=on go build -mod=vendor "-buildmode=pie" -ldflags "-X main.gitCommit=be6146b0a8471b02e776134119a2c37dfb70d414" -gcflags "" -tags "    " -o skopeo ./cmd/skopeo
go: inconsistent vendoring in /root/go/src/github.com/containers/skopeo:
	go.mod requires github.com/containers/skopeo  but vendor/modules.txt does not include it.
	run 'go mod tidy; go mod vendor' to sync
make: *** [binary-local] Error 1
```

This was working yesterday, today I am getting this error. Operator error? Or did something change....

comments.body:
(and just for the record, `go mod tidy`)
```
[root@my-computer]# go mod tidy
go: github.com/containers/image/v5@v5.0.0 requires
	github.com/docker/go-connections@v0.0.0-20180212134524-7beb39f0b969: invalid version: git fetch --unshallow -f https://github.com/docker/go-connections in /root/go/pkg/mod/cache/vcs/d4d85c75c05a73a49903227d5abc01a97e2af98bb40d3e0568d93c9e52de9e77: exit status 128:
	fatal: git fetch-pack: expected shallow list
```
Works on a new machine. Strange.
